texto: Anyone who has learned the Quran and holds it lovingly in his heart will 'value his nights when people are asleep, his days when people are given to excess, his grief when people are joyful, his weeping when people laugh, his silence when people chatter and his humility when people are arrogant'. In other words every moment of life will be precious to him, and he should therefore be 'gentle', never harsh nor quarrelsome, 'nor one who makes a clamour in the market nor one who is quick to anger'.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Ibn Mas'ud sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Anyone who has learned the Quran and holds it lovingly in his heart will 'value his nights when p…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, anger, chatter, útil em momentos de reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.

In other words every moment of life will be precious to him, and he should therefore be 'gentle', never harsh nor quarrelsome, 'nor one who makes a clamour in the market nor one who is quick to anger'.
